name: Thriller Bark
description: Thriller Bark is the world's largest pirate ship, a massive island-sized vessel shaped like a haunted mansion floating in the eternally foggy Florian Triangle. Built by Gekko Moria, one of the Seven Warlords of the Sea, it serves as both his base and a massive zombie factory. Using his Shadow-Shadow Fruit powers, Moria steals people's shadows and implants them into corpses, creating an army of undead servants. The ship is perpetually shrouded in darkness and mist, creating the perfect horror atmosphere. Inside are graveyards, gothic mansions, laboratories, freezers full of corpses, and countless zombies ranging from weak foot soldiers to powerful generals. It was here that the Straw Hats met Brook, the skeleton musician who'd been waiting 50 years to reunite with Laboon, and where Zoro demonstrated the ultimate loyalty with his 'Nothing Happened' moment.
history: Thriller Bark was constructed by the Gecko Pirates as a mobile fortress after Moria's devastating defeat in Wano Country against Kaido. Having lost his entire crew, Moria became obsessed with creating an immortal zombie army that would never die or betray him. He recruited the twisted genius Dr. Hogback, the ghost princess Perona, and the invisible creep Absalom to help run his operation. For years, they terrorized the Florian Triangle, stealing shadows from passing pirates and using their corpses to build Moria's zombie legion. The strongest zombie was Oars, a legendary giant whose body Moria found preserved in ice. When the Straw Hats entered the Florian Triangle, they encountered Brook first - a skeleton whose shadow had been stolen by Moria. The crew invaded Thriller Bark to retrieve their stolen shadows, leading to battles across the island-ship. The climax came when Moria inserted 1,000 shadows into his body and later into Oars's corpse with Luffy's shadow, creating an unstoppable monster. Luffy absorbed 100 shadows to become Nightmare Luffy, defeating Oars and Moria. Afterward, when Bartholomew Kuma arrived to eliminate the weakened crew, Zoro offered his life in exchange for Luffy's, taking all of Luffy's pain and damage while claiming 'Nothing Happened.' Brook finally joined as the crew's musician.
culture: Thriller Bark embodies a twisted horror-comedy aesthetic. Zombies retain their implanted shadow's personality, creating bizarre combinations of corpse bodies and living personalities. The island operates as Moria's gothic kingdom with strict hierarchy: the Mysterious Four (Moria, Absalom, Perona, Hogback) rule over zombie generals who command zombie soldiers. Zombie animals roam freely. The culture celebrates Halloween-esque darkness, with Victorian gothic architecture, horror movie tropes, and gallows humor. Despite the macabre setting, many zombies are comedic rather than frightening. The island's eternal night and fog create perpetual twilight. Salt is the ultimate weapon, as it purifies zombies by returning stolen shadows to their owners.

industries: Pirate ship raiding in Florian Triangle

transportation: Thriller Bark IS the transportation - a massive ship the size of an island. It sails through the Florian Triangle perpetually shrouded in fog. Internal transportation includes staircases through the mansion, bridges over chasms, underground passages to the freezer, and zombie-operated gates. The Mini Merry II (a smaller boat built by Franky from Merry's wood) was used by the Straw Hats to reach the island.

cuisine: Moldy corpse freezer contents (zombies don't need food)
quirks: Brook drinks milk to heal bones ('though I'm already dead')
Zombies eat out of habit, not hunger

notablePeople.name: Brook
notablePeople.description: A living skeleton and the sole survivor of the Rumbar Pirates. His Revive-Revive Fruit let him return to life, but he became a skeleton. After 50 years alone, he joined the Straw Hats to fulfill his promise to Laboon. His shadow was stolen by Moria and implanted in the samurai zombie Ryuma.

notablePeople.name: Gekko Moria
notablePeople.role: Warlord of the Sea / Shadow Master
notablePeople.description: A former fearsome pirate who became obsessed with creating an immortal zombie army after losing his crew to Kaido. His Shadow-Shadow Fruit lets him steal and manipulate shadows. Lazy and overconfident, relying on others to fight for him.

notablePeople.name: Perona
notablePeople.description: A gothic Lolita girl with the Hollow-Hollow Fruit, allowing her to create ghosts that cause extreme depression. Obsessed with cute things. Later becomes Mihawk's reluctant roommate and helps during timeskip.

notablePeople.name: Absalom
notablePeople.description: A invisible man with the Clear-Clear Fruit and grotesque animal body modifications by Hogback. Perverted and obsessed with Nami. Commands zombie soldiers and the zombie generals.

notablePeople.name: Dr. Hogback
notablePeople.description: A world-famous surgeon who faked his death to work with Moria. Creates zombies by modifying corpses and attaching shadows. Obsessed with resurrecting his deceased love, actress Victoria Cindry.

notablePeople.name: Ryuma
notablePeople.description: A legendary samurai from Wano whose corpse was animated with Brook's shadow. Possesses incredible sword skills and wields the legendary blade Shusui. Defeated by Zoro, who inherited Shusui.

notablePeople.name: Oars
notablePeople.description: The legendary 'Continent Puller' - a massive ancient giant whose preserved corpse was reanimated with Luffy's shadow. His combination of giant strength and Luffy's fighting style made him nearly unstoppable until the crew worked together.

notablePeople.name: Victoria Cindry
notablePeople.description: A famous actress who died in an accident and was resurrected as Hogback's zombie servant. Despite her shadow's personality, she sometimes shows traces of her original self's will, particularly her hatred of plates.

notablePeople.name: Monkey D. Luffy (Nightmare Form)
notablePeople.description: Luffy after absorbing 100 shadows, becoming a massive, blue-skinned powerhouse with incredible strength and speed. This temporary form let him defeat Oars and Moria.

notablePeople.name: Roronoa Zoro
notablePeople.description: Defeated the legendary Ryuma and claimed Shusui. Later took all of Luffy's pain from Kuma in the iconic 'Nothing Happened' moment, showing absolute loyalty and inhuman endurance.

notablePeople.name: Bartholomew Kuma
notablePeople.description: A Warlord sent to eliminate the Straw Hats after their victory. His Paw-Paw Fruit can repel anything, including pain and fatigue. Agreed to spare the crew if someone took Luffy's damage. Zoro volunteered.

notablePeople.name: Sanji
notablePeople.description: Used his knowledge that salt purifies zombies to great effect. Rescued Nami from Absalom's wedding attempt. Became invisible himself temporarily after Absalom's defeat.

notablePeople.name: Nami
notablePeople.description: Nearly forced into marriage with Absalom before being rescued. Used Perona's negative hollows concept to defeat enemies with weather attacks.

notablePeople.name: Usopp
notablePeople.description: Discovered that his natural negativity made him immune to Perona's ghosts. Defeated Perona through psychological warfare and his already rock-bottom self-esteem.

notablePeople.name: Lola
notablePeople.description: A zombie with a wild boar body who constantly proposes marriage. She helped the Straw Hats and gave Nami her mother's Vivre Card - later revealed to be Big Mom's daughter.

subLocations.name: Mansion Entrance
subLocations.description: The massive haunted mansion entrance with towering gates, gargoyles, and zombie guards. The first area the Straw Hats encountered upon boarding.

subLocations.name: Moria's Chamber
subLocations.description: Moria's massive throne room where he stores stolen shadows and commands his zombie army. The site of the final battle against Shadow Moria.

subLocations.name: Hogback's Laboratory
subLocations.description: Dr. Hogback's twisted operating theater where he stitches together corpses and creates zombies. Full of medical equipment and body parts.

subLocations.name: Perona's Wonder Garden
subLocations.description: Perona's cute, gothic room filled with stuffed animals, zombies, and her negative hollow ghosts. Where Usopp defeated her.

subLocations.name: Graveyard
subLocations.description: The massive graveyard where zombie soldiers emerge and where many battles occurred. Filled with tombstones, mausoleums, and fog.

subLocations.name: Freezer
subLocations.description: The giant freezer where corpses are stored before reanimation, including Oars's massive body. Intensely cold and full of preserved bodies.

subLocations.name: Oars Battle Zone
subLocations.description: The area where the entire Straw Hat crew fought the giant zombie Oars, requiring teamwork to defeat the massive threat.

subLocations.name: Nothing Happened Spot
subLocations.description: The isolated area where Zoro took all of Luffy's pain from Kuma and was found standing in a pool of blood, claiming 'Nothing happened.' One of the most iconic moments in One Piece.

items.name: Shadows
items.description: People's shadows stolen by Moria. Without a shadow, the victim will be vaporized by sunlight. Implanted in corpses to create zombies with the shadow owner's personality and skills.

items.name: Salt
items.description: The ultimate anti-zombie weapon. Putting salt in a zombie's mouth returns the stolen shadow to its owner and purifies the corpse.

items.name: Shusui
items.description: One of the 21 Great Grade Swords, wielded by the legendary samurai Ryuma. After defeating Ryuma's zombie, Zoro inherited this black blade.

items.name: Tone Dial
items.description: Brook's dial containing the final performance of the Rumbar Pirates singing 'Binks' Sake' - a treasure to bring to Laboon.

items.name: Mini Merry II
items.description: A smaller boat Franky built using wood from the Going Merry. The crew's emotional connection to their former ship.

items.name: Negative Hollows
items.description: Perona's ghosts that pass through people and make them extremely depressed and negative. Doesn't work on people who are already negative.

items.name: Luffy's Pain Bubble
items.description: A massive paw-shaped bubble containing all of Luffy's accumulated pain and fatigue from the arc. Zoro absorbed it entirely.

items.name: Thriller Bark Ship
items.description: The massive island-sized ship itself, the world's largest pirate vessel, designed as a floating haunted mansion.

items.name: Zombie Generals
items.description: Special zombies created from famous warriors' corpses with powerful shadows. Include samurai, captains, and legendary fighters.

items.name: Victoria Cindry's Plate
items.description: Plates that trigger Cindry's original personality to resist orders, showing her true self still exists beneath the zombie.

factions.name: Straw Hat Pirates
factions.description: The crew that invaded Thriller Bark to recover their stolen shadows and recruit Brook as their musician.

factions.name: Thriller Bark Pirates (Mysterious Four)
factions.description: The ruling elite of Thriller Bark who orchestrate the shadow theft operation and zombie army creation.

factions.name: Zombie Generals
factions.description: The most powerful zombies created from legendary warriors' corpses and skilled pirates' shadows.

factions.name: Zombie Army
factions.description: The massive army of animated corpses that serve Moria, ranging from weak soldiers to modified creatures.

factions.name: World Government (Intervention)
factions.description: Kuma was sent to eliminate the weakened Straw Hats but showed mercy after Zoro's sacrifice.

factions.name: Victim Pirates (Shadow Stolen)
factions.description: Pirates whose shadows were stolen, hiding on Thriller Bark and unable to see sunlight without dying.

events: Straw Hats enter the Florian Triangle
Shadows stolen by Moria - crew can't enter sunlight
Nami almost forced to marry Absalom
Bartholomew Kuma arrives to eliminate crew
Zoro offers his life for Luffy's
Zoro takes all of Luffy's pain
Sanji finds Zoro standing in blood pool
Revelation about Lola being Big Mom's daughter
Crew receives Lola's mother's Vivre Card

quickFacts: Moria is one of the Seven Warlords
Salt purifies zombies by returning shadows
Oars is an ancient giant zombie

videos.title: Nothing Happened - Zoro's Sacrifice
videos.description: The legendary moment where Zoro takes all of Luffy's pain from Kuma and claims 'Nothing happened.'

videos.title: Zoro vs Ryuma
videos.description: Zoro's duel against the legendary zombie samurai Ryuma to claim the sword Shusui.

videos.title: Nightmare Luffy
videos.description: Luffy absorbs 100 shadows and becomes a massive blue giant with overwhelming power.
